lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Reroll Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Use the fast 26-pull route, claim the web store rewards, and lock a start that carries story progress.

steps.title: Rush the opening setup
steps.description: Install the game, create your avatar, and clear the opening quickly. You can rename and re-customize later, so speed matters most during reroll loops.

steps.title: Finish the tutorial draw first
steps.description: Clear the tutorial and take the guaranteed Steel Sword pull before judging account quality. Real reroll value starts after tutorial completion.

steps.title: Link Square Enix account before spending
steps.description: Set age, link account, and prepare the official web store claim flow so your reroll run unlocks all launch tickets.

steps.title: Claim the full 26-pull package
steps.description: Collect the free web store reward, reopen the game, then claim all presents for 20 ticket pulls plus 6 gem pulls in one cycle.

steps.title: Prioritize Metal series keeps
steps.description: Metal Wing Boomerang and Metal Slime Sword are top launch keeps because they accelerate early progression and metal farming value.

steps.title: Use a clear keep line
steps.description: Safe stop is one Metal-series 3-star weapon. Ideal stop is one Metal-series 3-star plus one additional 3-star before counting redraw.

steps.title: Finish with guaranteed redraw
steps.description: After you pass your keep line, secure a second core weapon from redraw. Shiverstick is the most universal pickup for stable story clears.

steps.title: Reset cleanly when pulls miss
steps.description: Use Home > Menu > Title > Data Management to delete a failed run and restart from character creation for the next loop.

quickTips: A full reroll loop averages around 13 minutes when route discipline is consistent.
Do not skip account linking if you want the complete 26-pull cycle every run.
Metal-series starts scale better than random off-focus 3-star openings.
Lock your keep line before you start to avoid wasting time on weak boxes.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Your first 1-2 hours should focus on unlock thresholds, role concentration, and efficient resource timing instead of random grinding.

cards.name: Lock reroll and redraw before real progress
cards.description: Early damage comes from your weapon box, so finalize reroll keeps and complete guaranteed redraw before long story sessions.

cards.name: Push story before side farming
cards.description: Story clears unlock core systems and party slots, making it the best early-time investment.

cards.name: Focus one main vocation first
cards.description: Avoid splitting levels too wide. One main role around Lv.30 keeps early clears consistent and stable.

cards.name: Raise several weapons to Lv.30
cards.description: Broad Lv.30 weapon coverage beats over-investing one piece and improves elemental answers.

cards.name: Use Training Quests at progression walls
cards.description: Target weapon stones, armor stones, gold, and Metal Slime EXP instead of low-value wandering stages.

cards.name: Unlock expedition idle rewards early
cards.description: After Chapter 1 Normal, start dispatching immediately so offline time keeps feeding upgrades.

cards.name: Use Adventure Rank 7 and 8 checkpoints
cards.description: Rank 7 unlocks vocation changes, and Rank 8 unlocks the Vocation Panel for long-term growth.

cards.name: Prioritize party-wide panel nodes
cards.description: Whole-team gains stretch early growth stones further than narrow single-character branches.

cards.name: Save Metal Tickets and clear Rookie Panel
cards.description: Delay ticket spending until your box is stronger and finish Rookie Panel missions for a guaranteed 3-star equipment ticket.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Weapons
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Launch rankings reward safe range, fast special cycles, and reliable boss pressure. Build one farm weapon plus one burst answer.

items.name: Metal Wing Boomerang
items.description: Top farming and story weapon with huge coverage, fast special tempo, and safe ranged control.

items.name: Shiverstick
items.description: Most universal redraw option thanks to freeze control, long range safety, and smooth auto performance.

items.name: Metal Slime Sword
items.description: Premium reroll keep with metal-killer value, fast chain pressure, and strong single-target pacing.

items.name: Fire Blade
items.description: Direct burst option that stays useful for boss windows and simple high-damage rotations.

items.name: Crystal Claws
items.description: Excellent close-range burst and co-op DPS ceiling when you can maintain melee uptime.

items.name: Falcon Blade
items.description: Strong wind burst profile with mobility utility that keeps repeat clears fast and consistent.

items.name: Queen's Whip
items.description: Caster-friendly utility pick that supports attack-speed setups and staff-centric team tempos.

items.name: Platinum Plumes
items.description: Spell-seal utility gives it niche value in caster-heavy stages and controlled ranged clears.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Classes
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Class strength at launch follows weapon synergy and positional safety. Thief and Mage are the easiest all-mode winners.

solutions.name: Thief
solutions.description: Best all-purpose launch class with top boomerang and sword synergy plus strong movement tempo for farming routes.

solutions.name: Mage
solutions.description: Most stable ranged caster path with Shiverstick and Queen's Whip synergy in story and farming loops.

solutions.name: Warrior
solutions.description: Reliable melee anchor when you need durable pressure and safer boss attempts with sword and spear options.

solutions.name: Martial Artist
solutions.description: High close-range ceiling with crit-driven damage, rewarding active piloting and aggressive windows.

solutions.name: Priest
solutions.description: Best used for healing security and auto stability while your primary damage comes from another class.

managementTips: Pair class picks with your best 3-star weapon pool instead of forcing weak off-class setups.
Use Thief or Mage as your default progression anchor for smooth launch pacing.
Swap Warrior or Priest in when survival checks become the bottleneck.
Build one clear role per slot to avoid diluted team identity in harder runs.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Retry Gacha
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Retry Gacha is your guaranteed correction layer after reroll. Use it to secure a second core weapon and stabilize progression.

cards.name: Trigger redraw immediately after keep
cards.description: Once reroll meets your keep line, run guaranteed redraw before spending scattered tickets elsewhere.

cards.name: Shiverstick as universal first pick
cards.description: Shiverstick is the safest broad-use option for story clears, freeze control, and early auto comfort.

cards.name: Fallback redraw priorities
cards.description: Fire Blade, Falcon Blade, Sandstorm Spear, Crystal Claws, Platinum Plumes, and Queen's Whip are all workable launch finishes.

cards.name: Stop when account has two core 3-stars
cards.description: A Metal-series keep plus one solid redraw result gives enough stability to start efficient progression.

highlights: Avoid over-resetting after a qualified redraw result
Sync redraw choice with your intended main class
Lock account and move into story progression

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Gacha Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Use a clear pull plan: focus value banners, protect gem reserves, and map free tickets to your build goals.

regions.name: Launch Featured Banner
regions.description: Primary target for Metal-series opener value and early account acceleration.

regions.name: Guaranteed Redraw Banner
regions.description: Use after reroll keep confirmation to secure a second functional 3-star weapon.

regions.name: Ticket Pull Allocation
regions.description: Spend event and starter tickets in batches that match your active role plan instead of random split pulls.

regions.name: Gem Reserve Policy
regions.description: Hold a reserve for upcoming banner cycles so your account can react to stronger limited releases.

regions.name: Duplicate Handling
regions.description: Treat duplicates as progression material for core weapons, not as reasons to force a full strategy reset.

regions.name: Low-Spend Pull Rhythm
regions.description: Combine daily gems, events, and guaranteed value banners for steady growth without over-chasing.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Party
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Use preset team templates by scenario so your damage profile and utility stay consistent across story, farm, and co-op.

creatures.name: Story Progress Team
creatures.description: Thief + Mage + Warrior gives stable ranged pressure, AoE control, and frontline durability.

creatures.name: Metal Farming Team
creatures.description: Thief with Metal Wing Boomerang plus support slots prioritizes quick clear speed and reset pacing.

creatures.name: Boss Burst Team
creatures.description: Warrior or Martial Artist front with Fire Blade and Crystal Claws builds high short-window damage.

creatures.name: Safe Auto Team
creatures.description: Mage + Priest backbone with freeze and healing keeps auto runs stable over long sessions.

creatures.name: Co-Op Utility Team
creatures.description: Run one control weapon, one burst slot, and one sustain slot to cover mixed matchmaking groups.

creatures.name: Starter Budget Team
creatures.description: Any one strong 3-star carry plus two role-complement picks outperforms scattered off-plan upgrades.

items.name: Co-Op Structure

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Story Walkthrough
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Follow a chapter-first walkthrough with priority checkpoints so story progression stays smooth and efficient.

priorities.name: Chapter Push Order
priorities.description: Clear main story nodes first to unlock party slots, systems, and better farming routes.

priorities.name: Boss Gate Prep
priorities.description: Before each boss checkpoint, upgrade one core weapon and align class role buffs for stable clears.

priorities.name: Element Coverage
priorities.description: Maintain at least two elemental options so chapter enemies do not stall progression pacing.

priorities.name: Adventure Rank Tracking
priorities.description: Use rank milestones to decide when to pause story and complete unlock-oriented side tasks.

priorities.name: Resource Conversion Windows
priorities.description: Convert materials before chapter spikes so power jumps happen ahead of difficulty walls.

priorities.name: Stage Replay Discipline
priorities.description: Replay only high-value stages tied to your immediate upgrade plan, then return to mainline progress.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Memory
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Memory choices scale faster when grouped by role and element focus. Use this quick matrix to prioritize slots.

achievements.title: Burst Memory Set
achievements.description: Best for boss phases where short windows decide clear speed.

achievements.title: Crit Tempo Memory
achievements.description: Supports fast-hit classes and improves consistent DPS output.

achievements.title: Element Surge Memory
achievements.description: Boosts targeted element teams when chapter enemies have known weaknesses.

achievements.title: Guard Layer Memory
achievements.description: Raises frontline stability in high-pressure boss mechanics.

achievements.title: Recovery Pulse Memory
achievements.description: Adds sustain value for auto loops and long co-op sessions.

achievements.title: Mitigation Aura Memory
achievements.description: Reliable defensive pick when progression is blocked by incoming damage spikes.

achievements.title: Gauge Charge Memory
achievements.description: Improves special skill uptime and keeps rotation flow active.

achievements.title: Drop Boost Memory
achievements.description: Useful in repeat farming routes where material efficiency matters.

achievements.title: Co-Op Support Memory
achievements.description: Adds team value in matchmaking where role coverage is inconsistent.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Adventure Skills
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Adventure Skills and Blessings define build identity. These Dragon Quest Smash Grow FAQs cover practical combo logic.

faqs.question: How do Dragon Quest Smash Grow Adventure Skills shape a run?
faqs.answer: Adventure Skills decide your burst timing, sustain window, and clear speed profile, so skill routing should match your class role from the start.

faqs.question: What is a stable Dragon Quest Smash Grow Blessing combo for story?
faqs.answer: A balanced combo uses one damage amplifier, one control effect, and one sustain effect so story progression stays consistent.

faqs.question: Should Dragon Quest Smash Grow players chase one build theme only?
faqs.answer: Start with one theme for consistency, then flex one slot to answer stage mechanics or co-op team gaps.

faqs.question: Which Dragon Quest Smash Grow classes gain the most from skill chaining?
faqs.answer: Thief and Mage usually gain the highest immediate value because their kits convert tempo effects into clear speed quickly.

faqs.question: When should Dragon Quest Smash Grow players reroute a skill path?
faqs.answer: Reroute when boss mechanics or stage modifiers block your current build efficiency for multiple attempts.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Gems Farming
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Use repeatable gem routes so Dragon Quest Smash Grow pull planning stays stable across daily and weekly cycles.

faqs.question: What is the fastest Dragon Quest Smash Grow daily gem routine?
faqs.answer: Complete daily missions, claim login rewards, clear active event tasks, and collect present-box payouts at each reset.

faqs.question: How should Dragon Quest Smash Grow weekly gems be planned?
faqs.answer: Schedule weekly objectives early in the cycle so leftover days can be used for event overflow and bonus stages.

faqs.question: Which Dragon Quest Smash Grow events usually pay the most gems?
faqs.answer: Launch campaigns, limited challenge ladders, and milestone event boards usually produce the largest gem spikes.

faqs.question: Should Dragon Quest Smash Grow players spend gems immediately?
faqs.answer: Prioritize planned banner windows and keep an emergency reserve to avoid breaking long-term pull strategy.

faqs.question: Can Dragon Quest Smash Grow co-op play help gem income?
faqs.answer: Yes, co-op objective chains and event participation milestones can add steady gem income when rotated daily.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Daily Checklist
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Use this Dragon Quest Smash Grow daily checklist to convert short sessions into steady account growth.

settings.name: Claim login and inbox rewards
settings.description: Collect all day-start rewards first so ticket, gem, and stamina planning is accurate.

settings.name: Finish daily mission board
settings.description: Daily missions provide consistent materials and gem income that compound over time.

settings.name: Spend stamina on targeted stages
settings.description: Route stamina into weapon stones, class growth, or event nodes tied to your current bottleneck.

settings.name: Run at least one co-op session
settings.description: Co-op participation helps complete event chains and provides extra progression currency.

settings.name: Review gacha and code updates
settings.description: Check new codes and banner notices before ending the session to prepare next-day actions.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Leveling Guide
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Leveling speed depends on smart checkpoint timing, EXP routing, and class panel investment order.

entries.title: Early Leveling Window (Lv.1-30)
entries.description: Push story unlocks, then run focused training loops to get one core class and multiple weapons to Lv.30.

entries.title: Mid Leveling Window (Lv.30-50)
entries.description: Shift into rank-gated systems, panel upgrades, and event farming to maintain efficient power scaling.

entries.title: Metal Route Optimization
entries.description: Use metal-focused weapons and tickets when your build can clear quickly for higher EXP return per run.

entries.title: Class Panel Syncing
entries.description: Prioritize party-wide panel nodes before niche branches so each EXP session increases total team value.

entries.title: Preparation Before Boss Chapters
entries.description: Reserve upgrades for chapter spike points to avoid repeated wipe cycles and resource waste.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Co-Op Guide
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Dragon Quest Smash Grow co-op runs are strongest when teams lock role coverage, communication pace, and reward goals before queueing.

steps.title: Set role coverage before entering
steps.description: Assign one carry, one control slot, and one sustain slot so random mechanics do not break team tempo.

steps.title: Match weapon range to encounter style
steps.description: Use at least one safe-range weapon for wave control while melee burst slots handle boss windows.

steps.title: Sync ult and blessing timing
steps.description: Coordinate burst windows for boss phases so all players convert cooldowns into single clear spikes.

steps.title: Use quick communication rules
steps.description: Call out revive needs, phase swaps, and objective targets in short consistent phrases.

steps.title: Prioritize repeatable reward routes
steps.description: Run co-op stages with stable clear times and strong drop tables to maximize long-session value.

faq.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Dragon Quest Smash Grow.

questions.question: What is Dragon Quest Smash Grow about?
questions.answer: Dragon Quest Smash Grow is a mobile roguelite RPG focused on fast combat loops, weapon progression, class routing, and co-op quests.

questions.question: How should Dragon Quest Smash Grow players start progression?
questions.answer: Begin with a structured reroll route, lock a core weapon pair, and push story unlocks before broad side farming.

questions.question: Which Dragon Quest Smash Grow classes are easiest at launch?
questions.answer: Thief and Mage are the most consistent launch anchors due to weapon synergy, safety, and smooth farming pace.

questions.question: How can Dragon Quest Smash Grow players improve gacha value?
questions.answer: Use guaranteed redraw strategically, track codes and events, and keep gem reserves for planned banner windows.

questions.question: Does Dragon Quest Smash Grow include co-op content?
questions.answer: Yes. Dragon Quest Smash Grow supports up to four-player co-op with role-based team coordination and repeatable quest rewards.

questions.question: Where can Dragon Quest Smash Grow be downloaded?
questions.answer: Dragon Quest Smash Grow is available on the App Store and Google Play through official platform listings.
